8. Phase 8: The Fortress (Security & Multi-Tenancy) 🛡️¶

In an interstellar network, data leakage is a catastrophic failure. In this phase, we master The Fortress—Eden’s multi-layered security system that provides absolute tenant isolation and granular Row-Level Access Control (RBAC).

8.1. 🏗️ Multi-Tenancy Isolation¶

Eden uses a “Shared Schema, Filtered Data” approach to multi-tenancy. This allows for high performance while ensuring that Sector A can never access Sector B’s data.

8.1.1. 1. The TenantMixin¶

Add the TenantMixin to any model that belongs to a specific tenant (e.g., an Organization or Sector).

from eden.db.mixins import TenantMixin

class Drone(Model, TenantMixin):
    __tablename__ = "drones"
    serial_number: str = f()
    # 🔒 Automatic 'tenant_id' field is added and managed!

8.2. 🧱 Row-Level RBAC¶

Beyond multi-tenancy, you need to control which users within a tenant can perform specific actions. Eden does this via the __rbac__ policy.

from eden.db.access import AccessControl, AllowRoles, AllowOwner

class Document(Model, AccessControl):
    __tablename__ = "documents"
    
    __rbac__ = {
        "read": AllowOwner("author_id"),
        "update": AllowRoles(["admin", "commander"]),
        "delete": AllowRoles(["admin"]),
    }

This policy is enforced at the database level when you use the .for_user(user) QuerySet method.

8.3. 🔑 OAuth & Social Login¶

Eden provides first-class support for OAuth 2.0 via Google and GitHub.

8.3.1. 1. Registering Providers¶

from eden.auth.oauth import OAuthManager

oauth = OAuthManager()

oauth.register_google(
    client_id="YOUR_ID",
    client_secret="YOUR_SECRET",
    on_login=handle_social_login
)

# Mount the routes
oauth.mount(app, prefix="/auth/oauth")

8.3.2. 2. Login Flow¶

  • Initiate: GET /auth/oauth/google/login

  • Callback: GET /auth/oauth/google/callback (Automatic exchange)

8.4. 🚦 Security Middleware¶

To enable these “Elite” features, ensure the TenantMiddleware and AuthMiddleware are active in your app.py.

app.add_middleware("auth")
app.add_middleware("tenant")

8.5. ✅ Verification¶

To verify your Fortress:

  1. Test Tenant Isolation: Login as User A (Sector A) and try to access a Drone ID belonging to Sector B. Verify that a 404 Not Found or 403 Forbidden is returned.

  2. Test RBAC Enforcement: Login as a “Standard” user and try to delete a record. Verify that the action is blocked correctly.

  3. Test Automatic Filtering: Verify that Drone.query().all() automatically filters by the current authenticated tenant.

If your data is isolated and your permissions are absolute, your Fortress is 100% Verified. You are ready for Phase 9: Ecosystem Integrations.
